domain_knowledge: Spending and saving behavior can be used as proxies for Time preference of an individual.
workflow:
1. Chose BMI as dependent variable.
2. Time preference (independent) variables as 'DISSAVED' and 'SAMESAVE'.
3. Fit an OLS regression model and returned the model summary.

datasets.name: nls_bmi_processed.csv
datasets.description: This dataset is from the National Longitudinal Survey of Youth (NLSY79) includes variables such as gender, age, income, savings behavior, BMI, and racial background.

columns.GENDER: Gender of the Respondent (MALE or FEMALE)
columns.AGE: The age of the respondent in the year 1989
columns.AGE_2: Square of the age of the Respondent
columns.INCOME: The income of the respondent in the year 1989
columns.DISSAVED: A boolean variable that equals 1 if the respondent took more money out of than put into savings and equals 0 otherwise
columns.SAMESAVE: A boolean variable that equals 1 if the respondent's savings level did not change or the respondent had no savings, and is 0 otherwise
columns.BMI: Body mass index of the respondent calculated using the standard formula using the height and weight of the respondent using the height of 1985 and weight of 1989
columns.BLACK: A boolean variable that indicated if the respondent belongs to the black race or not
columns.HISPANIC: A boolean variable that indicated if the respondent belongs to the hispanic race or not

queries.question: What are the factors related to time preference associated with higher BMI in the 1989 data?
queries.true_hypothesis: Higher time preference associated with higher BMI for 1989 data. BMI is postively related with if person spent more than their saving with a coefficient 0.3596. BMI is also positively correlated with if the savings of a person remained unchaged with a coefficient 0.4858.

queries.question: How is higher time preference related to BMI in the 1989 data?
queries.true_hypothesis: Higher time preference associated with higher BMI for 1989 data. BMI is postively related with if person spent more than their saving with a coefficient 0.3596. BMI is also positively correlated with if the savings of a person remained unchaged with a coefficient 0.4858.

hypotheses.text: Higher time preference associated with higher BMI for 1989 data. BMI is postively related with if person spent more than their saving with a coefficient 0.3596. BMI is also positively correlated with if the savings of a person remained unchaged with a coefficient 0.4858.
